title: Crop-growing Skyscrapers
content_html:
Crop-growing skyscrapers

Crop-growing skyscrapers

A.

By the year 2050, nearly 80% of the Earth’s population will live in urban centres. Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase by about three billion people by then.An estimated 109 hectares of new land (about 20% larger than Brazil) will be needed to grow enough food to feed them, if traditional farming methods continue as they are practised today. At present, throughout the world, over 80% of the land that is suitable for raising crops is in use. Historically, some 15% of that has been laid waste by poor management practices.What can be done to ensure enough food for the world’s population to live on?

B.

The concept of indoor farming is not new, since hothouse production of tomatoes and other produce has been in vogue for some time.What is new is the urgent need to scale up this technology to accommodate another three billion people. Many believe an entirely new approach to indoor farming is required, employing cutting-edge technologies. One such proposal is for the ‘Vertical Farm’. The concept is of multi-storey buildings in which food crops are grown in environmentally controlled conditions. Situated in the heart of urban centres, they would drastically reduce the amount of transportation required to bring food to consumers.Vertical farms would need to be efficient, cheap to construct and safe to operate. If successfully implemented, proponents claim, vertical farms offer the promise of urban renewal, sustainable production of a safe and varied food supply (through year-round production of all crops), and the eventual repair of ecosystems that have been sacrificed for horizontal farming.

C.

It took humans 10,000 years to learn how to grow most of the crops we now take for granted. Along the way, we despoiled most of the land we worked, often turning verdant, natural eco-zones into semi-arid deserts. Within that same time frame, we evolved into an urban species, in which 60% of the human population now lives vertically in cities. This means that, for the majority, we humans have shelter from the elements, yet we subject our food-bearing plants to the rigours of the great outdoors and can do no more than hope for a good weather year. However, more often than not now, due to a rapidly changing climate, that is not what happens. Massive floods, long droughts, hurricanes and severe monsoons take their toll each year, destroying millions of tons of valuable crops.

D.

The supporters of vertical farming claim many potential advantages for the system. For instance, crops would be produced all year round,as they would be kept in artificially controlled, optimum growing conditions. There would be no weather-related crop failures due to droughts, floods or pests. All the food could be grown organically, eliminating the need for herbicides, pesticides and fertilisers.The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface.Although the system would consume energy, it would return energy to the grid via methane generation from composting non-edible parts of plants.It would also dramatically reduce fossil fuel use, by cutting out the need for tractors, ploughs and shipping.

E.

A major drawback of vertical farming, however, is that the plants would require artificial light.Without it, those plants nearest the windows would be exposed to more sunlight and grow more quickly, reducing the efficiency of the system. Single-storey greenhouses have the benefit of natural overhead light; even so, many still need artificial lighting. A multi-story facility with no natural overhead light would require far more. Generating enough light could be prohibitively expensive, unless cheap, renewable energy is available, and this appears to be rather a future aspiration than a likelihood for the near future.

F.

One variation on vertical farming that has been developed is to grow plants in stacked trays that move on rails. Moving the trays allows the plants to get enough sunlight. This system is already in operation, and works well within a single-story greenhouse with light reaching it from above: it is not certain, however, that it can be made to work without that overhead natural light.

G.

Vertical farming is an attempt to address the undoubted problems that we face in producing enough food for a growing population. At the moment, though, more needs to be done to reduce the detrimental impact it would have on the environment, particularly as regards the use of energy. While it is possible that much of our food will be grown in skyscrapers in future, most experts currently believe it is far more likely that we will simply use the space available on urban rooftops.

questions.question: Some food plants, including ___________ are already grown indoors.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–7

Complete the sentences below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“already grown indoors / example plants”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 2: “hothouse production of tomatoes and other produce…”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“tomatoes” là ví dụ cụ thể của cây trồng trong nhà → điền đúng 1 từ
=> Đáp án: tomatoes

_blockConfig.noteContent:
• Some food plants, including [1]  are already grown indoors.
• Vertical farms would be located in [2], meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.
• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce [3]
• The consumption of [4] would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.
• The fact that vertical farms would need [5] light is a disadvantage.
• One form of vertical farming involves planting in [6] which are not fixed.
• The most probable development is that food will be grown on [7] in towns and cities.
sentenceItems: • Some food plants, including [1]  are already grown indoors.
• Vertical farms would be located in [2], meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.
• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce [3]
• The consumption of [4] would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.
• The fact that vertical farms would need [5] light is a disadvantage.
• One form of vertical farming involves planting in [6] which are not fixed.
• The most probable development is that food will be grown on [7] in towns and cities.

questions.questionHtml:
• Some food plants, including 1_______ are already grown indoors.

• Vertical farms would be located in 2_______, meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.

• Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce 3_______

• The consumption of 4_______ would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.

• The fact that vertical farms would need 5_______ light is a disadvantage.

• One form of vertical farming involves planting in 6_______ which are not fixed.

• The most probable development is that food will be grown on 7_______ in towns and cities.

questions.question: Vertical farms would be located in ___________, meaning that there would be less need to take them long distances to customers.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“located / where built / city”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 2: “Situated in the heart of urban centres”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
“heart of urban centres” = trung tâm đô thị
=> Đáp án: urban centres

questions.question: Vertical farms could use methane from plants and animals to produce ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“methane / produce energy”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 4: “return energy to the grid via methane generation”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Methane → tạo ra energy
=> Đáp án: energy

questions.question: The consumption of ___________ would be cut because agricultural vehicles would be unnecessary.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“cut / vehicles / fuel”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn:
Paragraph 4: “reduce fossil fuel use, by cutting out tractors, ploughs and shipping”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
loại nhiên liệu dùng cho phương tiện nông nghiệp = fossil fuel
=> Đáp án: fossil fuel

questions.question: The fact that vertical farms would need ___________ light is a disadvantage.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “need / artificial / light”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 5: “A major drawback of vertical farming, however, is that the plants would require artificial light.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Trở ngại (drawback) của nông trại dọc là cây trồng yêu cầu (require) ánh sáng nhân tạo.
=> Đáp án: artificial

questions.question: One form of vertical farming involves planting in ___________ which are not fixed.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“planting”
“stacked trays”
“not fixed”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 6: “One variation on vertical farming that has been developed is to grow plants in stacked trays that move on rails.”
Bước 3: Phân tích:
Di chuyển trên đường ray (move on rails) nghĩa là không cố định. Cây được trồng trong các khay xếp chồng.
=> Đáp án: stacked trays

questions.question: The most probable development is that food will be grown on ___________ in towns and cities.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “probable development”
“urban rooftops”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 7: “...most experts currently believe it is far more likely that we will simply use the space available on urban rooftops.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Xu hướng có khả năng xảy ra cao nhất (far more likely) là tận dụng không gian trên sân thượng đô thị.
=> Đáp án: urban rooftops

questions.question: Methods for predicting the Earth's population have recently changed.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 8–13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 8–13 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“methods”
“predicting”
“population”
“changed”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 1: “Applying the most conservative estimates to current demographic trends, the human population will increase...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ đưa ra số liệu dự báo dựa trên xu hướng hiện tại, hoàn toàn không nói đến việc phương pháp dự báo có bị thay đổi hay không.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question: Human beings are responsible for some of the destruction to food-producing land.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword:
“human”
“responsible”
“destruction”
“land”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 3: “Along the way, we despoiled most of the land we worked, often turning verdant, natural eco-zones into semi-arid deserts.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: "We" (con người) làm tổn hại (despoiled) phần lớn đất đai canh tác biến chúng thành hoang mạc. Con người chính là tác nhân gây hủy hoại.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.question: The crops produced in vertical farms will depend on the season.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“vertical farms”
“depend”
“season”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Paragraph 4: “...crops would be produced all year round, as they would be kept in artificially controlled, optimum growing conditions.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cây trồng được sản xuất quanh năm (all year round) nhờ môi trường kiểm soát nhân tạo, tức là không phụ thuộc vào mùa màng. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.question: Some damage to food crops is caused by climate change.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“damage”
“crops”
“climate change”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 3: “However, more often than not now, due to a rapidly changing climate... destroying millions of tons of valuable crops.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Do khí hậu thay đổi nhanh chóng (due to a rapidly changing climate) dẫn đến thiên tai hủy hoại hàng triệu tấn nông sản.
=>  Đáp án: TRUE

questions.question: Fertilisers will be needed for certain crops in vertical farms.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“fertilisers”
“needed”
“vertical farms”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Paragraph 4: “All the food could be grown organically, eliminating the need for herbicides, pesticides and fertilisers.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Canh tác hữu cơ giúp loại bỏ hoàn toàn nhu cầu (eliminating the need) sử dụng phân bón hóa học. Thông tin trái ngược với câu hỏi.
Đáp án: FALSE

questions.question: Vertical farming will make plants less likely to be affected by infectious diseases.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: 
“less likely”
“affected”
“infectious diseases”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: 
Paragraph 4: “The system would greatly reduce the incidence of many infectious diseases that are acquired at the agricultural interface.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: 
Hệ thống này giảm mạnh tỷ lệ mắc (reduce the incidence) các bệnh truyền nhiễm, nghĩa là cây ít có nguy cơ bị nhiễm bệnh hơn.
Đáp án: TRUE
